content.problemStatement:
You are given a sequence of integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.

You should choose an arbitrary (possibly empty) subsequence of AAA and multiply each element of this subsequence by −1-1−1. The resulting sequence should satisfy the following condition: the sum of elements of any **contiguous** subsequence with length greater than 1 is strictly positive.

You should minimise the sum of elements of the resulting sequence. Find one such sequence with the minimum possible sum.
constraints: The sum of N for all test cases does not exceed 5 * 10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains a single integer N. The second line contains N space-separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing N space-separated integers B1, B2, …, BN. For each valid i, Bi must be equal to either Ai (the sign of this element did not change) or −Ai (the sign of this element changed).

sampleTestCases.explanation: Explanation: For each test case, we change the sign of the elements to make the sum of any contiguous subsequence with length greater than 1 strictly positive.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we will iterate over the array from left to right. For each element, we will check if changing its sign will make the sum of any contiguous subsequence with length greater than 1 strictly positive. If it does, we will change the sign of the element. Otherwise, we will keep the element as it is.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N) — where T is the number of test cases and N is the maximum number of elements in the array. This is because we are iterating over the array for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — where N is the maximum number of elements in the array. This is because we are storing the result in an array of the same size.
